aliases: Grilled Sea Bass Japanese Set Meal

english: Grilled Sea Bass Japanese Set Meal

food_subgroup: Japanese set meal with grilled fish, rice, and side dishes

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for shioyaki (Japanese salt-grilled fish set meal). FOODON_03302051 (grilled fish food product) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Fish, grilled) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for shioyaki sea bass set meal with rice and sides.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for shioyaki grilled sea bass set meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard shioyaki grilled sea bass set meal comprising approximately 150 g grilled sea bass fillet, 180 g steamed white rice, 100 g miso soup, and 20 g pickled vegetables, totalling approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: Sea bass, cooked (grilled); White rice, cooked; Miso soup — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods; supplemented with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Japanese set meal components
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a complete shioyaki grilled sea bass set meal. Values are estimated by weighted component assembly: approximately 33% grilled sea bass (USDA FDC #175159 sea bass cooked dry heat), 40% cooked white rice (USDA FDC #168878), 22% miso soup (USDA FDC #172457), and 5% pickled vegetables. Per-100g values are derived from the weighted blend of these components across a 450 g total serving.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — white rice (Japanese short-grain, cooked) GI ~50–54; grilled fish GI negligible
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a shioyaki grilled sea bass set meal. The GI of the set meal is dominated by the steamed white rice component. Japanese short-grain cooked white rice has a GI of approximately 50–54 per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Grilled sea bass and miso soup contribute negligible glycemic load. The composite GI of 50 is estimated using the carbohydrate-weighted average method across components, yielding gi_category 'low' (GI ≤ 55). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (50 × 71.1) / 100 ≈ 35.6 g, where carbohydrate per serving = 15.8 g/100g × 450 g = 71.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from miso soup and salt seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Each set meal provides approximately 1395 mg sodium (310 mg/100g × 450 g), which represents 58–70% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium miso soup or limit portion frequency.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic load from steamed white rice component
type2_diabetes.guidance: The set meal delivers a glycemic load of approximately 35.6 g per serving driven primarily by the white rice component (GI ~50); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider requesting a smaller rice portion or substituting with brown rice to reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean protein and omega-3 fatty acids from sea bass
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sea bass provides lean protein and omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids with low saturated fat (0.5 g/100g), making this set meal a heart-favourable choice; the primary cardiovascular concern is sodium from miso soup, which should be moderated in patients with established cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shioyaki Grilled Sea Bass Set (塩焼き鯛セット) is a traditional Japanese set meal (teishoku) centred on a whole or fillet portion of sea bass (suzuki) seasoned simply with salt and grilled over direct heat or in a fish grill until the skin is lightly charred and crisp while the flesh remains moist and flaky. The salt-grilling technique, known as shioyaki, is one of the most fundamental preparations in Japanese cuisine, prized for allowing the natural umami and delicate sweetness of fresh fish to shine without masking sauces. The set is served with steamed Japanese short-grain white rice, a bowl of dashi-based miso soup, and a small portion of pickled vegetables (tsukemono). Nutritionally, the meal provides a balanced macronutrient profile: approximately 9.2 g protein per 100 g from the sea bass, modest fat at 2.1 g per 100 g with a favourable unsaturated-to-saturated ratio, and carbohydrate primarily from the rice at 15.8 g per 100 g. The sea bass contributes meaningful vitamin B12 (0.9 mcg/100g), vitamin D (2.8 mcg/100g), selenium, and omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids. The primary clinical consideration is sodium: the miso soup and salt seasoning together contribute approximately 1395 mg sodium per full set, which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is moderate at approximately 35.6 g per serving, driven by the white rice component, making portion control of rice advisable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. This dish is widely served in Japanese restaurants in Singapore and is representative of washoku (traditional Japanese cuisine) dietary patterns.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a complete shioyaki grilled sea bass set meal as served at GyoGyo Funan. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for grilled sea bass, cooked white rice, and miso soup. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using Japanese short-grain white rice as the dominant glycemic contributor. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved databases and the assembly methodology is standard and documented.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated across a 450 g set meal (150 g grilled sea bass + 180 g steamed white rice + 100 g miso soup + 20 g pickled vegetables); per-100g values are derived from this weighted blend. 2. The food_name has been canonicalised to 'Shioyaki Grilled Sea Bass Set'; the original menu label '254D Shioyaki Grilled Sea Bass Set(D)' is preserved in aliases. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for grilled sea bass and cooked rice components; naturally zero in unprocessed fish and plain rice. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.7) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.6) are estimated from USDA FDC sea bass grilled dry heat fatty acid profile. 5. Sodium of 310 mg/100g reflects the significant contribution of miso soup (approximately 600 mg/100g miso soup) blended across the full set. 6. GI set to 50 (low, ≤ 55) based on carbohydrate-weighted composite of Japanese short-grain white rice GI ~50–54 (University of Sydney GI Database) and negligible GI contribution from fish and miso. 7. Halal status is assessed as likely halal (sea bass, rice, vegetables) but miso soup may contain dashi from bonito (fish-based, permissible) or kombu; no pork or alcohol ingredients typical in standard shioyaki set. 8. FoodOn FOODON_03302051 and FoodEx2 A00LC are closest_parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for this composite set meal. 9. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the component-level USDA SR Legacy entries used for estimation and are not derivable from the weighted blend without additional micronutrient data — however all such fields have been populated with best estimates from USDA FDC where available; no -1 values remain in the final record. 10. Price SGD 21.15 preserved from input (field survey data, GyoGyo Funan).
